metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4521
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig. 2 shows that OGT regulates the synaptic expression of AMPA receptors by immunoblot analysis of total and surface-expressed of AMPA receptor subunits. GluA2 and GluA3 are significantly downregulated in OGT KO animals.
Fig. 3 shows that deletion (Immuno-cytochemistry) of OGT leads to fewer surface GluA2 clusters.

15/02/2021 Dnyanada: The surface expression of GluA2 and GluA3 subunit was reduced in mature OGT KO cortical neurons. Figure 4 and 5 demonstrate a reduction in the number of mature spines in OGT KO neurons. However, as the paper does not causally (and chronologically) link these two effects, a separate annotation for the term "regulation of synapse maturation" was made (SynGO ID: 4525).. Experimental description: We developed a mouse model where OGT was deleted
genetically from mature brain neurons in vivo... (p. 1685) (cKO: PMID 26989246)
